name: Maple Pecan Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350°F (175°C).
2. In a large bowl, sift together 1 3/4 cups all-purpose flour, 1 teaspoon baking soda, and 1 teaspoon salt.
3. In another bowl, cream the softened butter until light and fluffy, then gradually add in 3/4 cup sugar, beating well after each addition.
4. Beat in 2 eggs (room temperature) one at a time, then beat for an additional 2 minutes.
5. Stir in 1 teaspoon vanilla extract and 1 teaspoon grated lemon peel.
6. Gradually stir the dry ingredients into the butter mixture, alternating with 3/4 cup of plain yogurt, beginning and ending with the dry ingredients.
7. Gently fold in 3/4 cup dried currants.
8. Pour the batter over the pecan pieces and sprinkle with ground mace in a greased 10-inch-diameter cake pan or an ovenproof glass baking dish (7 1/2 x 11 1/2 inches).
9. Bake until a tester inserted into the center comes out clean, about 55 minutes.
10. Remove from oven and let cool slightly before inverting onto a rack set over wax paper.
11. Serve warm, garnished with whipped cream if desired.
